channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

This large branch has fallen since our last walk last week.

item.title: Metal plate sticking out of manhole cover., 7th July
item.description:
I tripped on this and have badly hurt myself

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Boys playing football on the green, 7th July
item.description:
We at present have 9 school boys playing football on the green opposite our house there were there yesterday as well, they are hitting cars,windows, plants and flowers, knocking the leaves off the trees, screaming, kicking the ball really hard, our car has been hit twice and we have now moved it. ONE of the boys live on the estate the other8 do not, there used to be a sign no ball games on the green but has been taken down, can you please put up a sign as soon AS soon as possible and send someone round about 3.30 onwards to see what is happening and knock on doors to ask other tenants. they were there yesterday for nearly 2 hours and are there now as i am typing this,
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Aberford Gardens, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 93 glenarm overloaded bins, 7th July
item.description:
Bins overflowing no correct recycling being followed smell and waste awful

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glenarm Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drop kerb parking, 7th July
item.description:
Longton grove cars on school run drop off or pick up parking in front of drop kerb s

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 3, Longton Avenue, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unhitched and unattended commercial trailer left on the Public Highway, 7th July
item.description:
I'd like to report two horseboxes that have been left near the bridge on Thicket Road for an extended period and appear to have been abandoned. The first is a converted horsebox food trailer that has been left unhitched on the road for several months. It is not trading and has simply been occupying the space, creating an obstruction and detracting from the appearance of the area. Next to it is a second horsebox, which is in a very poor, dilapidated condition and is attached to a car. This trailer has remained in the same location for around a year. During that time, the car towing it appears to have moved only once before returning to the same spot. Both horseboxes appear to be using this location as long-term storage for commercial property on the public highway, rather than for any legitimate day-to-day use. Together, these trailers have become a significant eyesore, reduce the already limited parking available, particularly at weekends, and appear to be providing shelter for rats, which have been seen underneath both trailers. Could someone from Highway Enforcement please investigate whether these vehicles are being lawfully kept on the public highway and, if appropriate, arrange for their removal?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thicket Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Brick left near tree outside 55, 7th July
item.description:
Brick left on pavement near tree outside no 55. many weeds near tree. uneven pavement which is extremely dangerous when we get in and out of our car especially as we are getting a little older.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lingwood Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing tree, 7th July
item.description:
The original tree blew down 2 years ago and should have been replaced in April this year. Once again Bromley council are as much use as a chocolate tea pot. And please do not plant a cheap tree with messy berries on it. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 55, Broomfield Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bulky waste collection Reference 9689100 has not happened, 7th July
item.description:
Hello. I am not actually reporting flytipping, but waste items that should have been collected by Bromley Council - by arrangement - have not been taken. I booked and paid for a bulky waste collection (tv/stand, video and microwave) reference 9689100, to be done last Friday 03/07/2026. As of 14:30 today, 07/07/2026, the items are still outside my building. When I rang on Friday afternoon to report that the items were still there, I was told that some records Bromley has show that the items were collected on Friday at 08:21. They were not. I have also emailed Bromley and was told, yesterday, that the Neighbourhood Management Department have been made aware. Perhaps if I report them here, Bromley will collect the items, and quickly? Please help to get the broken, unsightly, unwanted items removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-62, Weighton Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 7th July
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waterdale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping alleyway Bellgrove Road between Edmund Road and Deepdene Road, 7th July
item.description:
Hi there is a load of rubbish at the back of the alley in Bellegrove Road Welling between Edmund Road and deepen road We are now getting a infestation of rats and mice from this. Please can this be cleared.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Bellegrove Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree, 7th July
item.description:
